key_insight: Foundational MLIPs trained at r2SCAN level can correct DFT formation energies toward experimental accuracy, and latent-feature delta-learning pushes the error below 50 meV/atom without extra DFT.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more accurate, fully open-source pathway for screening thermodynamic stability and prioritizing candidate materials in high-throughput discovery pipelines.
method: Compare MC3D thermodynamic stability data against OQMD, Materials Project, and experimental formation enthalpies.
Use PET-OMATPES foundational MLIP energies as a higher-accuracy replacement for GGA-based formation energies.
Train delta-learning models on latent MLIP features to further refine formation-energy predictions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports more than a 40% reduction in mean absolute error versus GGA and an end result below 50 meV/atom, comparable to experimental uncertainty.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and domain coverage of the foundational MLIP and on careful regularization to avoid distorting relative phase stability.
paper_title: Correcting DFT formation energies towards experimental accuracy using foundational MLIPs and latent-feature delta-learning
